title: Python requests Library — Auth, Retry, Pagination, Error Handling

confirmed_facts.fact: requests.Session() reuses TCP connections and persists headers/cookies across calls — always prefer Session over bare requests.get()
response.raise_for_status() raises requests.HTTPError for 4xx/5xx — use it after every call instead of manual status checks
requests.adapters.HTTPAdapter with urllib3.util.Retry provides built-in retry with backoff: Retry(total=3, backoff_factor=1, status_forcelist=[429, 500, 502, 503, 504])
Mount the retry adapter on both http:// and https:// prefixes: session.mount('https://', HTTPAdapter(max_retries=retry))
For 429 Too Many Requests, read the Retry-After header (seconds or HTTP-date) and sleep that long before retrying
response.json() raises ValueError/JSONDecodeError if body is not valid JSON — wrap in try/except when the API might return non-JSON error pages
For OAuth2 bearer token auth, use session.headers.update({'Authorization': f'Bearer {token}'}) once rather than passing per-request
Set timeout=(connect_timeout, read_timeout) on every request — never rely on the default (no timeout). Recommended: timeout=(10, 30)

implementation_notes:
Standard session setup with retry:
```python
import requests
from requests.adapters import HTTPAdapter
from urllib3.util.retry import Retry

def create_session(token: str | None = None) -> requests.Session:
    session = requests.Session()
    retry = Retry(total=3, backoff_factor=1, status_forcelist=[429, 500, 502, 503, 504], allowed_methods=['GET', 'POST', 'PUT', 'PATCH', 'DELETE'])
    session.mount('https://', HTTPAdapter(max_retries=retry))
    session.mount('http://', HTTPAdapter(max_retries=retry))
    if token:
        session.headers['Authorization'] = f'Bearer {token}'
    session.headers['Accept'] = 'application/json'
    return session
```

OData / link-based pagination pattern (Microsoft Graph, SharePoint, etc.):
```python
def paginate(session, url, params=None):
    results = []
    while url:
        resp = session.get(url, params=params, timeout=(10, 30))
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
        results.extend(data.get('value', []))
        url = data.get('@odata.nextLink') or data.get('next')
        params = None  # nextLink includes query params
    return results
```

Offset/limit pagination pattern (Qualys, Bitsight, generic APIs):
```python
def paginate_offset(session, url, page_size=100):
    results, offset = [], 0
    while True:
        resp = session.get(url, params={'offset': offset, 'limit': page_size}, timeout=(10, 60))
        resp.raise_for_status()
        data = resp.json()
        items = data.get('results', data.get('data', []))
        results.extend(items)
        if len(items) < page_size:
            break
        offset += page_size
    return results
```

Rate limit handling with Retry-After:
```python
import time

def rate_limited_get(session, url, **kwargs):
    resp = session.get(url, **kwargs)
    if resp.status_code == 429:
        wait = int(resp.headers.get('Retry-After', 60))
        time.sleep(wait)
        resp = session.get(url, **kwargs)
    resp.raise_for_status()
    return resp
```

Form-encoded token request (OAuth2 client_credentials):
```python
def get_token(tenant_id, client_id, client_secret, scope='https://graph.microsoft.com/.default'):
    url = f'https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/token'
    resp = requests.post(url, data={
        'grant_type': 'client_credentials',
        'client_id': client_id,
        'client_secret': client_secret,
        'scope': scope,
    }, timeout=(10, 30))
    resp.raise_for_status()
    return resp.json()['access_token']
```
caveats: urllib3 Retry does NOT retry on 429 by default — you must include 429 in status_forcelist explicitly
Retry with backoff_factor=1 waits 0s, 1s, 2s, 4s between retries — for APIs with strict rate limits, check Retry-After header instead
requests does not validate SSL certs from custom CAs by default — use session.verify='/path/to/ca-bundle.crt' or set REQUESTS_CA_BUNDLE env var
Never disable SSL verification (verify=False) in production code
